title: AgentDocument
description: The v2 AGTP Agent Document. Canonical identity record served at DESCRIBE on Form 1 and Form 1a URIs. Mirrors core.identity.AgentDocument. v1 documents are converted at load time by from_dict_v1_compat and emerge as in-memory v2 with document_version='v1-migrated'.

agtp_version.description: AGTP wire-protocol version this document is valid for. Today: '1.0'.

document_version.description: Schema version of the document itself. v2 is current. 'v1-migrated' identifies a v1 document auto-converted at load.

agent_id.description: Canonical Agent-ID. SHA-256 hex of the agent's Genesis document per draft-hood-independent-agtp-07.

name.description: Human-readable agent name. Not unique across servers; the agent_id is.

principal.description: Display name of the human or entity the agent acts on behalf of.

principal_id.description: Stable identifier of the principal (email, opaque id, organization handle).

description.description: One- or two-sentence description of the agent.

status.description: Operational lifecycle state. 'active' agents accept traffic; 'suspended' agents return 503; 'retired' agents return 410.

skills.description: Prose declarations of what this agent can do. The primary 'what does this agent do' surface. Replaces v1's 'capabilities' field.

requires.description: Structured declaration of methods, scopes, and wildcard policy the agent expects to handle inbound.

methods.description: AGTP verbs this agent is authorized to handle inbound. Methods absent here return 403 method-not-permitted-for-agent unless wildcards is true.

scopes.description: Authority scopes the agent expects to be presented for invocation.

wildcards.description: When true, the agent accepts any method (orchestrator pattern). Subject to per-server policies.wildcards_accepted.

scopes_accepted.description: Authority scopes this agent will honor on inbound requests.

issued_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp when the document was issued.

issuer.description: Domain or identifier of the issuing authority.
